info.title: ASPSMS WebAPI
info.description: The ASPSMS WebAPI enables sending and managing SMS messages. It offers balance inquiries, send/receive statistics, contact management, and more. The API supports basic and advanced SMS operations with extensive management for groups, templates, and jobs.

get.summary: Checks if User Credentials are valid
Returns the available Creditbalance from the current Account

post.summary: Calculates the estimated Credits, used prior a sending Operation

requestBody.description: List of MSISDNs, use ',' to specify mulitple occurences of one MSISDN, e.g. "0041791111111,5"

get.summary: Sends out one SMS with the used settings. For more advanced functionalities use the operation 'ASPSMSSendSMS'

parameters.name: MSISDN
parameters.description: Mobile Number to which the SMS is sent.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.description: Operation which should be invoked. Use "SendTextSMS", otherwise it will not work

parameters.name: Originator
parameters.description: Originator of the SMS. Originator can be either numeric or alphanumeric (11 chars)

parameters.name: DeferredDeliveryTime
parameters.description: If empty, SMS will be sent immediately. If specified, SMS will be sent on particular Date/Time. Example: 06/30/2019 18:32:00

parameters.name: TransactionReferenceNumber
parameters.description: TransactionReferenceNumber, if specified with this parameter can be queried about the delivery result of the SMS

post.summary: Sends out one SMS immediately or with deferred delivery time with advanced settings. Please check Properties of the abstract Datatype 'SMSData'. Important: For 'Operation', use the value 'SendTextSMS', otherwise it will not work

requestBody.description: SMSData, Holds all data related to the SMS Sending

post.summary: Deactivate pending SMS with MSISDNs. Submitted, but not yet sent SMS can be deactivated

500.description: If there is an internal server error

200.description: Returns the number of SMS deactivated successfully

post.summary: Deactivate pending SMS with TransactionReferenceNumber. Submitted, but not yet sent SMS can be deactivated

500.description: If there is an internal server error

200.description: Returns the number of SMS deactivated successfully

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets statistical information about how many SMS at what days have been submitted
This information can be used to e.g. create a navigation bar which includes the number of SMS for every single day.

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary: Gets detailed Sendlog information for all SMS that have been submitted on a specific date. If Month or Day are ommited, Query returns data for complete Year, resp. Month (Max. 50000 records)

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary: Gets detailed Sendlog information for all SMS to one specific MSISDN

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets detailed Sendlog information for all SMS with a specific TransactionReferenceNumber.
The TransactionReferenceNumber has to be specified when sending the SMS

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets detailed Sendlog information for all SMS with a specific JobID.
The JobID has to be specified when sending the SMS

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary: Gets detailed Inbox information for all SMS that have been received. Any combination of the Filter criterias Year, Month, Day, MSISDN and Originator is allowed. Max Number of Records can be specified but is limited to 5000 Records per Query

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets statistical information about how many SMS at what days have been received
This information can be used to e.g. create a navigation bar which includes the number of SMS received for every single day.

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets statistical information about how many SMS at what days have been received
This information can be used to e.g. create a navigation bar which includes the number of SMS received for every single day.

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets statistical information about how many SMS at what days have been received
This information can be used to e.g. create a navigation bar which includes the number of SMS received for every single day.

tags: Sendlog, Statistics and Two-Way Incoming Traffic
get.summary:
Gets statistical information about how many SMS at what days have been received
This information can be used to e.g. create a navigation bar which includes the number of SMS received for every single day.
Gets statistical information (number of delivered, failed, etc.) for all MSISDNs
Gets the Ignorelist, SMS to these MSISDNs will be ignored and not processed

post.summary: Does add one or more MSISDNs to Ignorelist
Does remove one or more MSISDNs from Ignorelist

get.summary: Reads the Phonebook. If MSISDN is specified, only the corresponding records will be returned

post.summary: Updates one or more Phonebook Entrys
Adds one or more Phonebook Entrys
Upserts one or more Phonebook Entrys
Upserts one or more Phonebook Entrys. Additional information is assigning the Phonebook Entrys to specific groups
Deletes one or more Phonebook Entrys

requestBody.description: List of Phonebook IDs to be deleted

get.summary: Reads the Phonebook Groups

post.summary: Upserts one or more Phonebook Group Entrys
Deletes one or more Phonebook Group Entrys

requestBody.description: List of Phonebook Group IDs to be deleted

get.summary: Get all Grouprelations
Get all Grouprelations for one specific MSISDN

post.summary: Updates Grouprelations
Adds new Grouprelations
Deletes Grouprelations
Deletes all Grouprelations of one specific Phonebook ID

get.summary: Get all Jobs

post.summary: Upserts Jobs
Deletes Jobs

get.summary: Get all Templates

post.summary: Upserts Templates
Deletes Templates

get.summary: Gets a list of all possible Operators

TransactionReferenceNumber.description: Optional TransactionReferenceNumber, should be unique. For later inquiry of e.g. Delivery Notifications
Optional TransactionReferenceNumber, should be unique. For later inquiry of e.g. Delivery Notifications

MessageData.example: Hello World!, Use this for a personalized Message

Originator.description: Originator or SenderID of the Message. Can be alphanumeric (max. 11 Chars) or numeric
